events.title: Lebanon mourns three journalists killed in Israeli strikes
events.description: Hundreds of mourners gathered at a cemetery south of Beirut on Sunday to attend the funeral of journalists Ali Shoeib, Fatima Ftouni, and Mohammed Ftouni, who were killed in an Israeli strike a day earlier. Israel has acknowledged carrying out the attack, claiming that Shoeib was affiliated with a militant group, but the incident has sparked widespread outrage within Lebanon, where officials have condemned it as a clear breach of international law. According to the Committee to Protect Journalists, the deaths bring the number of media workers killed in Lebanon since the start of the Israel–Gaza war to eleven, as Eliza Herbert reports.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Lebanon mourns three journalists killed in Israeli strikes

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 27 March: Humanitarian crisis deepens across the region
events.description: The war in the Middle East continues, with attacks causing further terror and suffering, deepening the humanitarian crisis across the region. The UN announced the launch of a task force to meet the immense needs. In Geneva, diplomats at the Human Rights Council have been discussing the school strike in Iran’s Minab that killed more than 100 children. Stay with us for live updates on this and from UN agencies providing relief. App users can follow coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 27 March: Humanitarian crisis deepens across the region

events.title: World News in Brief: Airstrikes continue in Ukraine, refugees return to DR Congo, emergency aid for the ‘Dry Corridor’
events.description: The latest wave of Russian attacks across Ukraine have reportedly killed and injured civilians and damaged critical infrastructure in Dnipro, Odesa, Poltava, Zaporizhzhia and other regions.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 World News in Brief: Airstrikes continue in Ukraine, refugees return to DR Congo, emergency aid for the ‘Dry Corridor’

events.title: Philippines declares ‘national energy emergency’ and boosts coal power as Iran war grinds on
events.description: President’s declaration allows officials to tackle fuel hoarding or profiteering, while energy secretary says country will lean more heavily on coal Middle East crisis – live updates The Philippines president, Ferdinand Marcos, has declared a state of “national energy emergency” as a result of the Middle East war , which his administration said posed “an imminent danger of a critically low energy supply”. The state of emergency, which will initially last for a year, was declared just hours after the country’s energy secretary said the Philippines planned to boost the output of its coal-fired power plants to keep electricity costs down as the war wreaks havoc with gas shipments. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Philippines declares ‘national energy emergency’ and boosts coal power as Iran war grinds on

events.title: Myanmar: WFP reports fragile recovery one year after deadly earthquake
events.description: A “new wave of global instability is hitting Myanmar at the worst possible moment,” a UN official in the country warned on Friday, as increases in fuel, food, and fertilizer prices due to the ongoing Middle East conflict push vulnerable families closer to hunger one year after a devastating earthquake.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Myanmar: WFP reports fragile recovery one year after deadly earthquake

events.title: Middle East war: Attacks on vital healthcare, evacuation strike fears
events.description: Almost one month since Israeli and US airstrikes on Iran began, sparking a wider regional war, UN agencies and partners on Friday highlighted the terror among civilians fleeing bombardment, with “no safe space” to go.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Middle East war: Attacks on vital healthcare, evacuation strike fears

events.title: Qatari news channel AI Araby condemns attack on its office
events.description: Iran’s parliamentary speaker has cautioned the United States against launching a ground invasion, warning that Tehran could intensify attacks on US allies, according to state media. Meanwhile, an American-born Israeli soldier has been killed during fighting in southern Lebanon, officials confirmed. At the same time, senior diplomats from Saudi Arabia, Turkey and Egypt are gathering in Pakistan to advocate for an end to the conflict involving Iran, as the Trump administration awaits Tehran’s official reply to a 15-point peace proposal. France24 correspondent Reza Sayah reports the latest from Tehran, Iran.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Qatari news channel AI Araby condemns attack on its office

events.title: Over a dozen U.S. soldiers injured in attack on Saudi base as Iran-backed Houthis enter war
events.description: An Iranian strike on an air base in Saudi Arabia wounded at least 15 U.S. service members. Israel also said it intercepted a missile launched from Iran-backed Houthi rebels in Yemen.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Over a dozen U.S. soldiers injured in attack on Saudi base as Iran-backed Houthis enter war

events.title: Day of zero waste: International Chef Massimo Bottura speaks to France 24
events.description: Monday marks the UN’s International Day of Zero Waste, an occasion aimed at raising awareness about sustainability, with this year’s spotlight on food. Each year, roughly one billion tonnes of edible food - nearly one-fifth of what is available to consumers - ends up being discarded, a reality that affects both communities and the environment. To explore this issue further, we turn to Modena, Italy, to speak with Massimo Bottura, a globally renowned chef, leading advocate against food waste, and Goodwill Ambassador for the UN Environment Programme.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Day of zero waste: International Chef Massimo Bottura speaks to France 24

events.title: Pakistan offers to host US-Iran talks as Tehran warns ground troops would be 'set on fire'
events.description: Pakistan on Sunday said it would soon host talks between the United States and Iran over the monthlong war, positioning itself as a mediator as fighting intensifies across the region, global energy routes are shaken and fears grow of a broader conflict involving Israel, Lebanon and key maritime chokepoints.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Pakistan offers to host US-Iran talks as Tehran warns ground troops would be 'set on fire'

events.title: The week in pictures: Massive daytime attack on Ukraine, 'No Kings' and France's adieu to Jospin
events.description: France held its national tribute to former prime minister Lionel Jospin, Russia launched one of its largest daytime attacks on Ukraine, and protesters across the US and some world capitals held a third round of "No Kings" mass protests against President Donald Trump: here's a look back at the week's most striking images.

events.title: France foils bomb attack outside Bank of America in Paris
events.description: French police on Saturday stopped an apparent bomb attack outside a Bank of America branch in Paris. The French counter-terrorism prosecutor's office said it was investigating a "terrorist criminal conspiracy". Nicholas Rushworth and our colleagues from FRANCE 2 report.
events.ticker_label: 💥 France foils bomb attack outside Bank of America in Paris

events.title: Persian Gulf crisis impacting food security, FAO warns
events.description: The intensifying conflict in the Persian Gulf “has triggered one of the most rapid and severe disruptions to global commodity flows in recent times,” the Chief Economist with the UN Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO) said on Thursday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Persian Gulf crisis impacting food security, FAO warns

events.title: Two drone strikes on civilian targets kill 28 people in Sudan
events.description: Market in North Darfur and truck carrying civilians in North Kordofan hit as civil war approaches fourth year At least 28 civilians have been killed in two separate drone strikes in Sudan, according to health workers, as the country’s brutal civil war between the army and the paramilitary Rapid Support Forces (RSF) approaches its fourth year . A strike hit a market in the town of Saraf Omra, in North Darfur state, on Wednesday, killing “22 people, including an infant, and injuring 17 more”, a health worker at the local clinic told Agence France-Presse (AFP). Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Two drone strikes on civilian targets kill 28 people in Sudan

events.title: North Korea tests high-thrust engine in long-range missile push
events.description: The tests come days after Kim Jong Un accused the US of global "state terrorism and aggression," in an apparent reference to the Iran war.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 North Korea tests high-thrust engine in long-range missile push

events.title: Jeremy Bowen: Trump is waging war based on instinct and it isn't working
events.description: One month into the conflict in Iran, Trump's gut-instinct approach is not proving effective, writes the BBC's international editor.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Jeremy Bowen: Trump is waging war based on instinct and it isn't working

events.title: Father of slain 20-year-old killed by illegal immigrant issues stark warning after Sheridan Gorman killing
events.description: Joe Abraham warns more deaths will follow in Illinois after his daughter Katie and Loyola student Sheridan Gorman were allegedly killed by illegal immigrants.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Father of slain 20-year-old killed by illegal immigrant issues stark warning after Sheridan Gorman killing

events.title: Syria: UN Commission documents grave violations in July 2025 escalation in Sweida
events.description: More than 1,700 people were killed and nearly 200,000 more displaced in the July 2025 massacres in Sweida, Syria, UN human rights investigators said on Friday in a report that called for greater action towards accountability.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Syria: UN Commission documents grave violations in July 2025 escalation in Sweida

events.title: Australia politics live: Jim Chalmers says working from home ‘makes a lot of sense’ during fuel crisis but government will avoid Covid-style measures
events.description: Treasurer says Australians should use fuel responsibly rather than cancel Easter road trips. Follow updates live Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Fuel rationing not inevitable, says Bowen Bowen says the government has learned lessons from the Covid pandemic, and won’t be doing 9am press conference every day “saying what you can and can’t do tomorrow.” No, I don’t think so, Mel, but of course, governments are meeting, including today on prudent planning. Continue reading...

events.title: World News in Brief: 273 million out of school, deadly attacks on Ukraine, migratory species in danger
events.description: The number of children and young people out of school worldwide has climbed for the seventh consecutive year, reaching 273 million, according to a new report from the UN education agency, UNESCO.

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 25 March: All eyes on Strait of Hormuz; war is ‘out of control’, UN chief warns
events.description: It’s day 26 of war in the Middle East. The UN chief says with the conflict now totally out of control, diplomacy must prevail. Ongoing strikes in Israel and Iran have included intensifying Israeli attacks against Hezbollah targets in Lebanon, while some US troops are heading to the region, the Pentagon has confirmed. Meanwhile, Iran has told the UN maritime agency that the Strait of Hormuz remains open to “non-hostile” ships not associated with the US and Israel. UN News app users can follow coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 25 March: All eyes on Strait of Hormuz; war is ‘out of control’, UN chief warns

events.title: Two humanitarian aid boats heading to Cuba have gone missing, Mexico says
events.description: Navy searching for two boats that left Isla Mujeres last week bound for Havana with nine crew members of different nationalities on board Mexico’s navy said on Thursday it had activated a search-and-rescue operation in the Caribbean to locate two sailboats carrying humanitarian aid to Cuba after the vessels failed to arrive in Havana as scheduled. In a statement, the navy said the two boats left Isla Mujeres, in the Mexican Caribbean state of Quintana Roo, last week bound for Havana with nine crew members of different nationalities on board. Continue reading...

events.title: Massive protests against Trump across US
events.description: Huge crowds protested Saturday against US President Donald Trump's actions and the war in Iran in "No Kings" rallies across the US and in Europe. Minnesota took centre stage, with thousands of people standing shoulder-to-shoulder to celebrate resistance to Trump's aggressive immigration enforcement. France 24 International Affairs Editor Kethevane Gorjestani tells us more.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Massive protests against Trump across US

events.title: Houthis open new front in Iran war: Will Yemeni group block Bab al-Mandeb?
events.description: Blockade of strait, one of the world's busiest maritime routes, would prove disastrous for global economy.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Houthis open new front in Iran war: Will Yemeni group block Bab al-Mandeb?

events.title: Venezuelans deported by US detail fresh claims of torture and abuse at El Salvador mega-prison
events.description: Petition seeks accountability from Salvadorian authorities over human rights violations at notorious Cecot facility A group of 18 Venezuelan men whom the US expelled a notorious Salvadorian mega-prison are demanding that Salvadorian authorities be held internationally accountable for violation of human rights – detailing new allegations of torture, sexual assault and medical neglect. A new petition, filed on Thursday before the Inter-American Commission on Human Rights, alleges that El Salvador violated the human rights of these men, who were expelled to El Salvador’s Terrorism Confinement Center (Cecot) last year without charge. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Venezuelans deported by US detail fresh claims of torture and abuse at El Salvador mega-prison

events.title: UN chief calls for immediate release of 118 detained UN staff
events.description: UN Secretary-General António Guterres has called for the immediate release of the 118 UN staff currently detained worldwide as attacks against the United Nations increase with 179 personnel arrested or detained last year alone.

events.title: Nigeria takes its place on world stage in quest to become regional superpower
events.description: Nigeria and UK look to strengthen trade and economic ties amid growing calls from Africa and Caribbean for reparative justice “There are chapters in our shared history that I know have left some painful marks,” King Charles said during a state banquet to welcome the Nigerian president, Bola Tinubu, to the UK, in a year in which the monarch is expected to come under renewed pressure to make a formal apology for transatlantic slavery and colonialism. But while demands grow from African and Caribbean nations for the UK to further reparative justice, Nigeria and the UK are looking to the future of global trade. Continue reading...

events.title: Three arrested in Paris after attempted bomb attack outside Bank of America
events.description: France's anti-terrorism prosecutor's office said it had immediately taken over the investigation.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Three arrested in Paris after attempted bomb attack outside Bank of America

events.title: Yemen's Houthi rebels enter the fighting in the Middle East
events.description: At least 15 U.S. military personnel were wounded in an Iranian attack on Saudi Arabia and Yemen's Houthi rebels entered the war with an attack on Israel.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Yemen's Houthi rebels enter the fighting in the Middle East

events.title: Extreme weather in Afghanistan leaves 17 people dead, authorities say
events.description: Afghan authorities say that severe flooding, a landslide and thunderstorms in parts of Afghanistan have left 17 people dead and 26 injured over the last 24 hours
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Extreme weather in Afghanistan leaves 17 people dead, authorities say

events.title: The race against time to destroy Iran’s illicit nuclear weapons program heats up amid fresh strikes
events.description: The IDF says it struck Iran's Arak heavy water plant, a key plutonium site, as experts warn Natanz and Isfahan still hold highly enriched uranium.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 The race against time to destroy Iran’s illicit nuclear weapons program heats up amid fresh strikes

events.title: ‘We can insure the ship, but we cannot insure a human life.’
events.description: International Maritime Organization chief Arsenio Dominguez warns of 20,000 seafarers stranded in the Strait of Hormuz.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 ‘We can insure the ship, but we cannot insure a human life.’

events.title: Farmers plead for tax breaks, diesel guarantees and help buying fertiliser as national cabinet meets on fuel crisis
events.description: State and federal leaders due to discuss assistance for business sectors but petrol rationing not expected to find backing Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Farmers say the federal government must help them with tax breaks and underwriting fertiliser purchases to survive the fuel crisis, with Monday’s national cabinet expected to discuss more assistance to businesses amid ballooning petrol prices. Federal and state governments have remained tight-lipped about what would be on the meeting’s agenda but state premiers have urged the Albanese government to take a stronger national coordination role in the crisis. Continue reading...

events.title: Iranian missile hits an airbase in Saudi Arabia, injuring U.S. troops
events.description: A U.S. official said several U.S. service members were wounded in the strike and some aircraft were apparently damaged as well. Iran has targeted U.S. bases throughout the region since the war began.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iranian missile hits an airbase in Saudi Arabia, injuring U.S. troops

events.title: Belgium's state-owned railway shines light on Holocaust role
events.description: A Holocaust survivor and the son of a Nazi collaborator have shared their experiences at Brussels' Train World museum. Talking to students, they warned of the dangers of forgetting history.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Belgium's state-owned railway shines light on Holocaust role

events.title: Anti-war protests in Israel: Hundreds gather in Tel-Aviv
events.description: Hundreds gathered in Tel Aviv on Saturday to protest the U.S.-Israeli war against Iran, the latest in a series of weekly demonstrations since the conflict began a month ago. Participation now appears to be growing, prompting a tough response from Israeli authorities, who moved to disperse the protesters.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Anti-war protests in Israel: Hundreds gather in Tel-Aviv

events.title: A Houthi missile attack on Israel stokes fears of renewed Red Sea shipping strikes
events.description: Iranian-backed Houthi rebels in Yemen have launch missiles toward Israel and raised fears of new attacks on Red Sea shipping
events.ticker_label: 🪖 A Houthi missile attack on Israel stokes fears of renewed Red Sea shipping strikes

events.title: Sudan: Hospital strike highlights surge in drone attacks on civilians
events.description: The death toll from a horrific attack on a hospital in Sudan’s Darfur has risen further, amid a “sharp increase” in drone attacks against civilians this year, UN agencies said on Tuesday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Sudan: Hospital strike highlights surge in drone attacks on civilians

events.title: At least 2 students killed, several injured in school bus crash in Tennessee: 'A parent's worst nightmare'
events.description: Two students were killed and seven were injured in a school bus crash on Highway 70 in Carroll County, Tennessee, during a Kenwood Middle School field trip.

events.title: US troops brace for ‘hit-and-run’ guerilla attacks as 82nd Airborne deploys to Iran, military analyst warns
events.description: The 82nd Airborne Division deployment to the Middle East is intended to pressure Iran into accepting U.S. ceasefire terms, military analyst Michael Eisenstadt says.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US troops brace for ‘hit-and-run’ guerilla attacks as 82nd Airborne deploys to Iran, military analyst warns

events.title: Maldives tells UK it does not recognise Chagos Islands deal with Mauritius
events.description: The Maldives is threatening international legal action to press its claim for control of the Chagos Islands.

events.title: Houthis open new front against Israel, is Red Sea shipping at risk?
events.description: The Houthis in Yemen have launched their first attacks on Israel, opening a new front in the month-long regional war.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Houthis open new front against Israel, is Red Sea shipping at risk?

events.title: Australia will be left with no submarines if it abandons Aukus, senior defence official warns
events.description: Malcolm Turnbull asks defence department official what Australia would do if the promised Virginia-class and Aukus-class submarines don’t arrive Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Australia will be left with no submarines if it abandons the Aukus deal with the US and UK, a senior defence official has warned, declining to publicly countenance an alternative plan if Australia’s promised nuclear-powered fleet does not arrive under Australian command. “Defence has been directed to pursue Aukus and we are pursuing Aukus and that’s our plan. I would not venture into the space about ‘Plan B’ or ‘Plan C’,” defence department deputy secretary, Hugh Jeffrey, told a Sovereignty and Security Forum in Canberra on Friday. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Australia will be left with no submarines if it abandons Aukus, senior defence official warns

events.title: Mediation talks in Islamabad: Middle East powers meet to discuss how to end the fighting
events.description: The United States is plotting a ground attack on Iran even as it claims to be engaging in diplomatic efforts, Iranian parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf said Sunday, as more American marines arrive in the region. The Israeli army earlier announced the death in combat of a fifth soldier in south Lebanon. Adjunct Senior Lecturer at Lunds Univeristy in Stockholm, Rouzbeh Parsi, shares further insights on US-Iran negotiations.

events.title: Gaza peace doubts deepen as attention shifts to Iran
events.description: With the world focused on the Iran war, there is increasing uncertainty about what happens next for Gaza.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Gaza peace doubts deepen as attention shifts to Iran

events.title: From the Iran war to the US petrol pumps
events.description: 30 days into the war in Iran, the key Strait of Hormuz is still partially closed, making prices of oil rise, considerably affecting businesses and citizens all around the world. Business owner John Catsimatidis decrypts this situation with France 24's Gavin Lee.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 From the Iran war to the US petrol pumps

events.title: Africa is hurting again from a global crisis it had no part in starting
events.description: Africa is hurting again from a global crisis it had no part in starting

events.title: Middle East war: UN initiatives support mediation efforts, ‘lifesaving’ fertiliser shipments
events.description: Just hours after war broke out in the Middle East last month, United Nations Secretary-General António Guterres warned the Security Council that the fighting risked “igniting a chain of events that no one can control in the most volatile region of the world.”
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Middle East war: UN initiatives support mediation efforts, ‘lifesaving’ fertiliser shipments

events.title: World News in Brief, Cuba blockade hits healthcare, Haiti’s ‘vortex of violence’, deadly Djibouti shipwreck
events.description: Hospitals are just the latest vital public services to be hit in Cuba, more than a month since Washington took measures to block oil supplies from entering the Caribbean nation.

events.title: Iranian attacks across Gulf continue as major industrial sites hit
events.description: A number of people are said to have been injured after attacks on aluminium sites in the UAE and Bahrain.

events.title: Qatari TV station hit in Iran
events.description: Qatar’s Al Araby network condemns an attack on its office in Tehran, which injured at least 10 people.

events.title: Netanyahu orders military to expand invasion of southern Lebanon
events.description: As troops push towards Litani River, mourners gather for the funeral of three slain journalists killed in line of duty.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Netanyahu orders military to expand invasion of southern Lebanon

events.title: Trump’s trip to meet Xi Jinping in China rescheduled for May due to Iran war
events.description: US president says he will host Chinese leader in a reciprocal visit later this year Middle East crisis – live updates Donald Trump will meet Xi Jinping in May during the US president’s first visit to China in eight years, a closely watched trip that had been postponed due to the Iran war. Trump was initially slated to travel next week, but will now visit Beijing on 14 and 15 May, he wrote in a post on Truth Social on Wednesday. Trump said he would host the Chinese leader in a reciprocal visit in Washington later this year. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Trump’s trip to meet Xi Jinping in China rescheduled for May due to Iran war

events.title: ‘Entirely wiped out’ crops, buildings destroyed and weeks of recovery as cyclone damage assessed
events.description: Critical Western Australia agriculture region counting cost of brutal cyclone as flooding risk persists for low-lying communities Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast An agricultural region that supplies about 60% of Western Australia’s fresh winter produce is assessing damage as authorities continue work on Sunday to restore power to a popular tourist town hit hard by Cyclone Narelle. The food-bowl region near Carnarvon, about 900km north of Perth, provides 80% of of the state’s bananas. Meanwhile, flooding risk remains in the state’s low-lying communities. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ ‘Entirely wiped out’ crops, buildings destroyed and weeks of recovery as cyclone damage assessed

events.title: Victoria and Tasmania get free public transport in fuel crisis but NSW and WA to keep collecting fares
events.description: Allan government says measure is temporary as energy shock from Middle East conflict sees petrol prices soar Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Public transport will be free in Victoria for a month and in Tasmania until July, in an effort to encourage people to switch from driving and to alleviate the surge in fuel demand. However, the NSW and Western Australian governments will not follow suit, with NSW’s transport minister saying it needs to “keep our powder dry” to deal with a crisis that may last much longer than a month. Continue reading...

events.title: Fact check: Fake satellite images distort Middle East conflict from above
events.description: As the Iran war intensifies, satellite imagery has become a key battleground in the information war. Manipulated or AI‑generated aerial images are increasingly blurring the line between fact and fiction on the ground.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Fact check: Fake satellite images distort Middle East conflict from above

events.title: Iran war: Houthis claim 2nd attack on Israel in 24 hours

events.title: Missing Cuba-bound boats carrying aid arrive safely in Havana
events.description: Two sailboats had gone missing while sailing from Mexico to Havana to alleviate the humanitarian crisis in Cuba. The Mexican Navy located the vessels and escorted them to port.

events.title: As many as 70 Brits detained in UAE over Iran war images, claims campaign group

events.title: Ukraine: danger is only increasing, warns UN human rights office

events.title: Woman helping cancer-stricken friend executed in alleged carjacking attack: 'Heard a scream'
events.description: Texas woman Marietta Allison was fatally shot in an alleged carjacking in Houston while caring for her best friend battling stage four ovarian cancer.

events.title: They bought an abandoned 'ghost house' in the Japanese countryside
events.description: He'd spent years backpacking around the world, and Japanese traveler Daisuke Kajiyama was finally ready to return home to pursue his long-held dream of opening up a guesthouse.

events.title: Scientists film whale giving birth while other whales work together to help her
events.description: Female named Rounder surrounded by family members when about to give birth to her second calf Scientists have managed to film a sperm whale giving birth while other female whales worked together to support the mother and her newborn. A team from Project Ceti, an international effort seeking to understand how whales communicate, was in a boat near a pod of 11 whales off the coast of the Caribbean island of Dominica on 8 July 2023. Continue reading...

events.title: Caster Semenya shapes up for new battle with the International Olympic Committee
events.description: For Caster Semenya, the new Olympics rules are not about providing clarity and protecting women's sport but caving into political pressure, and are discriminatory.

events.title: Gaza: Commitment to US-backed plan crucial to recovery, Security Council hears
events.description: As tensions escalate in the Middle East, the international community must not lose sight of the situation in Gaza, an official with US President Donald Trump’s Board of Peace across the shattered enclave said on Tuesday in his first appearance in the UN Security Council.

events.title: Spanish government's Jimmy Gracey autopsy claims stump forensic expert: 'problem for me'
events.description: A forensic scientist says claims that Jimmy Gracey sustained bruises over hours are "implausible," raising doubts about Spain's accidental death ruling.

events.title: North Korea tests solid-fuel missile engine as Kim boosts threat to US mainland
events.description: Kim Jong Un oversaw a new solid-fuel rocket engine test, state media reports, as North Korea pushes to upgrade its long-range strategic strike capabilities.

events.title: Israeli police block Latin Patriarch from Palm Sunday mass in Jerusalem

events.title: Missing aid boats have safely reached Cuba, US confirms
events.description: Two convoy vessels that were supposed to get to Havana by Wednesday have made it to Cuba, says US Coast Guard Two sailing boats that went missing while carrying humanitarian aid to Cuba have safely reached the Caribbean island, the US Coast Guard said on Friday. Earlier in the day Cuba’s president, Miguel Díaz-Canel, had said his country would do everything it could to save the people on the two boats that disappeared while travelling to Cuba from Mexico. Continue reading...

events.title: Iran: 3.500 US troops arrive in the region

Iranian parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf earlier accused Washington of plotting a ground attack on Iran even as it claims to be engaging in diplomatic efforts. Senior fellow in Geopolitics, Royal United Services Institute, Dr. H.A. Hellyer shares further insights.

events.title: UN’s landmark slavery ruling energises African Union’s fight for reparations
events.description: • UN votes to describe slave trade as ‘gravest crime against humanity’ Despite resistance from states who had role in chattel slavery, many feel this is an idea whose time has come John Mahama knows a thing or two about beating the establishment. On Wednesday, less than two years after completing a remarkable comeback as Ghana’s president with a landslide defeat of the ruling party candidate, he rallied the world to ratify a landmark vote against transatlantic chattel slavery, despite major opposition from the same western entities that drove it for centuries. The resolution to declare the practice as “the gravest crime against humanity” passed with a decisive majority at the UN general assembly and has been largely welcomed across Africa. Yet the details of the tally reveal a world still deeply divided on the gravity of the sin of enslaving more than 15 million people as chattel over the course of 400 years. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ UN’s landmark slavery ruling energises African Union’s fight for reparations

events.title: ‘They can reach me wherever’: China using financial tactics to coerce people who flee, says report
events.description: UK urged to tackle transnational repression, as dissidents say Beijing has targeted them with tax bills and other threats “I didn’t feel safe, even though I’m not based in Hong Kong any more,” said Christopher Mung Siu-tat after getting tax bills from Hong Kong authorities. “The regime can reach me by their long arms wherever I am.” Siu-tat, the executive director at the Hong Kong Labour Rights Monitor, a UK-based NGO, fled Beijing’s sweeping national security laws years ago. The letters are the latest example of a series of transnational repression (TNR) tactics the 54-year-old has faced in recent years. Continue reading...

events.title: ‘The violence of racist tyranny’: African Guernica goes on display alongside Picasso masterpiece
events.description: Piece by late South African artist Dumile Feni is part of new series History Doesn’t Repeat Itself, But It Does Rhyme On the second floor of the Reina Sofía, in the very spot where Picasso’s Guernica was first exhibited when it arrived in the Madrid museum 34 years ago, there now hangs a smaller, near-namesake of the Spanish artist’s most famous work. While African Guernica, which was drawn by the late South African artist Dumile Feni in 1967, may lack the scale of Picasso’s masterpiece, its depth, anger and unnerving juxtaposition of man and beast, light and dark, and innocence and cruelty, are every bit as disturbing. Continue reading...

events.title: Australia refuses to say how many Chinese nationals are arriving by boat, saying it may damage bilateral relations
events.description: Exclusive: Indonesia reports growing number of attempts by Chinese nationals to organise boat journeys, as Australian authorities refuse to reveal details Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The Australian government has refused to reveal how many Chinese nationals have arrived in Australia by boat since 2024, saying that disclosing the figure may harm relations with other countries. However, reports by Indonesian police show that there has been a consistent trend of Chinese nationals attempting to reach Australia through Indonesia as an alternative to “zouxian”, or “walking the line” – the illegal migration route through Central America to the US via the Darién Gap between Colombia and Panama. Continue reading...

events.description: Now that the Fall-Winter 2023 catwalks have been disassembled, it's clear one trend was more pervasive than any collective penchant for ruffles, pleated skirts or tailored coats.

events.title: Michael Jordan's 1998 NBA Finals sneakers sell for a record $2.2 million
events.description: In 1998, Michael Jordan laced up a pair of his iconic black and red Air Jordan 13s to bring home a Bulls victory during Game 2 of his final NBA championship — and now they are the most expensive sneakers ever to sell at auction. The game-winning sneakers sold for $2.2 million at Sotheby's in New York on Tuesday, smashing the sneaker auction record of $1.47 million, set in 2021 by a pair of Nike Air Ships that Jordan wore earlier in his career.

events.title: Here's the real reason to turn on airplane mode when you fly
events.description: We all know the routine by heart: "Please ensure your seats are in the upright position, tray tables stowed, window shades are up, laptops are stored in the overhead bins and electronic devices are set to flight mode."

events.title: 'I was up to my waist down a hippo's throat.' He survived, and here's his advice
events.description: Paul Templer was living his best life.

events.title: Relaxed entry rules make it easier than ever to visit this stunning Asian nation
events.description: Due to its remoteness and short summer season, Mongolia has long been a destination overlooked by travelers.

events.title: The most beautiful sections of China's Great Wall
events.description: Having lived in Beijing for almost 12 years, I've had plenty of time to travel widely in China.

events.title: US embassy in Mexico prompts outrage with AI video promoting ‘self-deportation’
events.description: AI-generated footage depicts group of men performing a corrido, singing phrases including ‘return to your roots’ An AI-generated video from the US embassy in Mexico encouraging migrants to “self-deport” has sparked disbelief and outrage online. The video posted this week on official embassy social media accounts depicts a group of men wearing black caps and sporting tattoos performing a kind of traditional Mexican ballad known as a corrido . events.title: The iconic South African theater that took on apartheid
events.description: South Africa's iconic Market Theatre, born in the darkest days of apartheid and a force for change, is celebrating its 50th anniversary.

events.title: Cuban Americans face scrutiny for sending goods back to the island
events.description: Cuban Americans who ship goods to relatives on the island are now seen as propping up Cuba's communist regime as the economy there continues to deteriorate.

events.title: Senate votes to fund most of DHS. And, Trump extends Iran's deadline to reopen strait
events.description: The Senate has voted to fund most of the Department of Homeland Security. And, President Trump extends the deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: Russia wants to legalize cars stolen in EU
events.description: Vehicles stolen in the EU may soon be legally registered in Russia. One German police union fears this could lead to an increase in car thefts in Europe.

events.title: Germany news: Prognosis 'not good' for humpback whale
events.description: Hopes that a whale stranded in shallow waters off the Baltic coast can be guided back to the ocean are fading. Meanwhile, hundreds escaped from a nightclub that became engulfed in flames.

events.title: Nestle says thieves stole 12 tons of KitKat chocolate bars
events.description: "We've always encouraged people to have a break with KitKat," the company said, "but it seems thieves have taken the message too literally."

events.title: 🔴 Netanyahu orders military to 'further expand' Lebanon security buffer zone
events.description: No description available.

events.title: Senegal celebrate AFCON title at Stade de France friendly amid controversy
events.description: Senegal marked their Africa Cup of Nations triumph in controversial fashion at the Stade de France on Saturday despite being stripped of the title in favor of Morocco. The Teranga Lions went on to beat Peru 2-0 in a friendly, but the dispute rumbles on. The Senegalese Football Federation has lodged an appeal with the Court of Arbitration for Sport following CAF’s decision.

events.title: The law is coming for social media, with the science still uncertain
events.description: The law is finally catching up with social media. This week, a California jury found Meta and Google liable for addicting a child to their platforms. On Tuesday, the French Senate will vote to ban under-15s from social media, with other countries making similar plans. Is humanity saved? Or are things more complicated than that?

events.title: Israeli police bar top Catholic figure in Jerusalem from holding Palm Sunday mass at Holy Sepulchre
events.description: Cardinal Pierbattista Pizzaballa, the top Catholic figure in Jerusalem, was barred from entering the Church of the Holy Sepulchre to celebrate Palm Sunday mass, according to his office, in what Italy's Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni labelled an "offence to the faithful". French President Emmanuel Macron said

events.title: Two new suspects arrested over foiled Bank of America bomb plot in Paris
events.description: Two suspects have been arrested in connection with an apparent bomb plot targeting the Paris headquarters of Bank of America, French prosecutors said Sunday, a day after police detained a man who was about to set off a homemade explosive device outside the US bank.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Two new suspects arrested over foiled Bank of America bomb plot in Paris

events.title: Quarantined astronauts take part in Q&A with days to go until historic moon mission
events.description: The countdown is on until NASA's first moon mission in more than half a century, and the quarantined astronauts who'll be travelling into space have taken part in a Q&A.

events.title: Pope Leo XIV visits Monaco to urge its people to use their faith and wealth for good
events.description: Pope Leo XIV has arrived in the cosmopolitan Mediterranean principality of Monaco to encourage its people to use their Catholic faith and wealth for good

events.title: Indonesia starts implementing social media restrictions for children under 16
events.description: Indonesia has begun implementing a new government regulation approved earlier this month that bans children younger than 16 from access to digital platforms that could expose them to pornography, cyberbullying, online scams and addiction

events.title: A stranded whale in Germany’s Baltic Sea weakens as hopes of its return to the Atlantic fade
events.description: A stranded humpback whale in Germany’s Baltic Sea looks weaker, and experts fear it won't be able to find its way back to the Atlantic

events.title: North Korea conducts engine test for missile capable of targeting US mainland
events.description: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un has observed a test of a high-thrust, solid-fuel engine for weapons
events.ticker_label: 🪖 North Korea conducts engine test for missile capable of targeting US mainland

events.title: Mongolia ruling party picks a new prime minister candidate
events.description: Mongolia’s ruling party has chosen a new prime minister candidate after its former primer minister resigned

events.title: Kidnapped and tortured on an infamous migration route to Saudi Arabia
events.description: An Ethiopian man describes how he was tortured by human traffickers as he went in search of his nephew on a now infamous migration route from the Horn of Africa through Yemen to Saudi Arabia.

events.title: Haitian gangs expand reach as police are accused of ‘summary executions’
events.description: Deadly gangs in Haiti are expanding their reach to include control over key sea and road routes as police in the beleaguered Caribbean island nation are being accused of using “unnecessary and disproportionate lethal force and summary executions.”

events.title: ’90 Day Fiance’ alum’s boyfriend beats Boca Bash attempted murder case he rips as ‘ridiculous’
events.description: Cole Goldberg was acquitted of attempted murder charges tied to a 2022 Boca Bash incident involving a "90 Day Fiancé" personality after a bench trial.
events.ticker_label: 💥 ’90 Day Fiance’ alum’s boyfriend beats Boca Bash attempted murder case he rips as ‘ridiculous’

events.title: Gas thief creeps into learning center, drains van serving students with disabilities
events.description: A thief was caught on camera siphoning gas from a transport van at a Clovis, California, school serving nearly 190 students who have intellectual disabilities.

events.title: Repeat offender allegedly shoots teen, pistol-whips victim after prank sparks violent retaliation
events.description: A Tacoma man with four prior felony convictions allegedly shot a teen in the chest after a water balloon hit his car, then robbed the group of $100.

events.title: Fox News True Crime Newsletter: 'Lovers' Lane' breakthrough, Gilgo Beach suspect's plea, Jimmy Gracey's ruling
events.description: Stay up to date with the Fox News True Crime Newsletter, which brings you the latest cases ripped from the headlines, from crime to courts, legal and scandal.

events.title: North Korean laborers describe brutal forced labor in Russia: 'Working like a cow, earning nothing'
events.description: A North Korean forced labor survivor describes earning $800 a month but keeping only $10, with passports seized and no way to leave Russian work sites.

events.title: Zelenskyy claims US tied Ukraine security guarantees to giving up Donbas, White House denies
events.description: Zelenskyy claimed U.S. security guarantees for Ukraine are tied to ceding Donbas to Russia, but the White House says those claims are false.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Zelenskyy claims US tied Ukraine security guarantees to giving up Donbas, White House denies
